_meta.purpose: Curated source-of-truth for the lifestyle -> chronic disease -> mortality risk-pathway visualization. Read by tools/public_health/build_risk_pathways_data.py and emitted to src/data/wellbing/riskPathways.js.
_meta.scope: United States, recent years. Effect sizes are headline numbers from the cited paper for the comparator noted on each edge.
_meta.gbdNote: Edges sourced from GBD 2021 are tagged with sourceId 'gbd-2021-usa'. The raw IHME bundle is not yet wired into the local DuckDB pipeline; until it is, the GBD-tagged values are taken from the published vizhub tables and re-checked at the accessed date below.

gbd-2021-usa.label: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 — United States
gbd-2021-usa.notes: Risk-cause attribution for 2021. PAFs are taken from the GBD 2021 risk-cause results for the United States.

nchs-2024.label: CDC/NCHS Multiple Cause-of-Death Public Use File, 2024
nchs-2024.notes: U.S. mortality counts and percentages by cause for 2024.

lee-2014-jama.label: Lee 2014 (JAMA) — Effect of physical inactivity on major non-communicable diseases worldwide
lee-2014-jama.notes: Population-attributable fraction for physical inactivity across CHD, T2D, breast and colon cancer, and all-cause mortality.

moore-2016.label: Moore 2016 (JAMA Internal Medicine) — Leisure-time physical activity and 26 types of cancer
moore-2016.notes: Pooled cohort of 1.4M adults; high vs low leisure activity hazard ratios for 13 of 26 cancer sites.

hamer-2009.label: Hamer & Chida 2009 (Psychological Medicine) — Physical activity and risk of neurodegenerative disease
hamer-2009.notes: Meta-analysis of 16 prospective studies. Highest vs lowest physical-activity quartile.

predimed-2018.label: PREDIMED 2018 (NEJM) — Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease with a Mediterranean diet
predimed-2018.notes: Randomized trial; Mediterranean diet plus extra-virgin olive oil or nuts vs low-fat control. Primary endpoint hazard ratios.

afshin-2019.label: Afshin 2019 (Lancet) — Health effects of dietary risks in 195 countries
afshin-2019.notes: GBD 2017 dietary risks analysis. Population attributable fractions for cardiovascular and metabolic outcomes by country.

uspstf-crc-2021.label: USPSTF 2021 — Screening for Colorectal Cancer recommendation
uspstf-crc-2021.notes: Modeling estimates from CISNET show 25–30% colorectal cancer mortality reduction at recommended screening intervals.

marmot-2013.label: Marmot 2013 (Br J Cancer) — Independent UK Panel on Breast Cancer Screening
marmot-2013.notes: 20% relative reduction in breast cancer mortality from invitation to screening among women aged 50–70.

nlst-2011.label: NLST 2011 (NEJM) — Reduced Lung-Cancer Mortality with Low-Dose Computed Tomographic Screening
nlst-2011.notes: 20% relative reduction in lung cancer mortality with annual low-dose CT in heavy smokers aged 55–74.

cappuccio-2011-chd.label: Cappuccio 2011 (Eur Heart J) — Sleep duration predicts cardiovascular outcomes
cappuccio-2011-chd.notes: Meta-analysis of 15 prospective cohorts. Short sleep RR 1.48 for CHD; long sleep RR 1.38.

cappuccio-2010-t2d.label: Cappuccio 2010 (Diabetes Care) — Quantity and quality of sleep and incidence of type 2 diabetes
cappuccio-2010-t2d.notes: Meta-analysis of 10 prospective studies. Short sleep RR ~1.55; long sleep RR ~1.65 for incident T2D.

sabia-2021.label: Sabia 2021 (Nature Communications) — Association of sleep duration in middle and old age with incidence of dementia
sabia-2021.notes: Whitehall II cohort. Persistent short sleep (≤6h) at age 50 hazard ratio ~1.30 for dementia.

kivimaki-2012.label: Kivimäki 2012 (Lancet) — Job strain as a risk factor for coronary heart disease
kivimaki-2012.notes: Pooled analysis of 13 cohorts. Job strain hazard ratio 1.23 (1.10–1.37) for incident CHD.

yusuf-2004-interheart.label: Yusuf 2004 (Lancet) — INTERHEART: psychosocial risk factors for acute myocardial infarction
yusuf-2004-interheart.notes: Case-control study of AMI in 52 countries. Psychosocial stressors odds ratio 2.67 (2.21–3.22).

cohen-2012-pnas.label: Cohen 2012 (PNAS) — Chronic stress, glucocorticoid receptor resistance, inflammation, and disease risk
cohen-2012-pnas.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
cohen-2012-pnas.notes: Mechanistic evidence linking chronic stress to inflammatory dysregulation. Effect-size estimates approximate.

edges.note: Inactive adults vs adults meeting activity guidelines.
Type-2 diabetes incidence; consistent with Helmrich-style cohorts.
Pooled HR for colorectal, breast, and 11 other site-specific cancers.
Pooled estimate across dementia and cognitive decline outcomes.
Primary endpoint: composite of MI, stroke, CV death.
U.S. share of T2D burden attributable to dietary risks (low whole grains, high processed meat, high sugar).
Largely colorectal cancer attributable to processed meat and low fiber.
Colorectal screening at recommended intervals; pooled CISNET model.
Mammography for women aged 50–70.
Low-dose CT screening in current/former heavy smokers.
Pooled cohort meta-analysis. Long sleep also raises risk (RR 1.38).
Incident T2D over follow-up.
Whitehall II 25-year follow-up.
Pooled IPD analysis of 13 European cohorts.
Acute MI case-control across 52 countries.
Mechanistic — chronic stress drives glucocorticoid receptor resistance and pro-inflammatory phenotype.
Atherosclerotic processes underlie the great majority of heart-disease deaths.
Ischemic strokes (atherosclerotic) account for the bulk of stroke mortality.
All cancer deaths.
Alzheimer disease accounts for the named neuro-degenerative mortality bucket.
Diabetes mellitus deaths.
Share of heart-disease deaths attributable to high fasting plasma glucose.
Inflammatory and immune dysregulation share of chronic lower-respiratory mortality.
